what_it_does: Defines a single role (develop_option) that produces one complete, lens-biased approach to a design decision, plus the closed lens vocabulary and a flat Pydantic output schema, so the same role can be fanned by run_items over multiple lenses.
observations: Implements the MAP half of COMPOSITIONS ⑩ (option-panels) by fanning one role over per-lens input units.
The lens, question, and constraints ride inside the unit dict placed at ctx['utterance']; input_addresses stays the default ('utterance',).
Module-level LENSES tuple declares the closed vocabulary: mvp-first, risk-first, reuse-first, framework-first, radical-recompose.
DevelopOptionOut has six scalar string fields, including an echoed lens key so downstream scoring can key by bias.
The prompt instructs the model to adopt exactly one lens bias and return only JSON.
model_binding requires chat and json, sets default_model to None, and notes the panel runs on the resident swarm without a GPU window.
mode_scope is {'panel'}, a context cast beyond listening, with no effect on the default listening cast.
The role is advisory only: it develops options but never picks the fork; scoring/recommendation is delegated to score_options.
Extensive docstring commentary references verify_lens.py, register_element.py, screen_reader.py, and AGENTS.md rule 9.
standouts: Axis-inversion design: one role file becomes N independent panelists because the lens is carried in each input unit.
Closed lens vocabulary declared as module-level data rather than buried in prose.
Flat scalar output shape intended for side-by-side comparison by a reducer.
Explicit refusal to declare lens/question/constraints as separate inputs to avoid dead declared-extras on the default compose path.
conventions: Uses Pydantic BaseModel for the output schema.
Uses a ROLE dict with id, label, description, prompt_template, output_schema, input_addresses, op, trigger, model_binding, mode_scope, rules, render_hint.
Echoes a key input field (lens) in the output schema.
Declares a mode_scope cast beyond listening.
Includes long explanatory docstrings that link the file to composition patterns and sibling roles.
concerns: The prompt's JSON example is reuse-first and fairly specific, which may subtly bias other lenses toward similar framing.
The LENSES tuple is repeated in the prompt prose, creating a drift risk if one is updated without the other.
There is no runtime enforcement that the supplied lens value is a member of LENSES.
The heavy inline documentation may become stale relative to the actual runtime behavior of run_items and mode_scope.
notes: A reusable, fan-out MAP panelist that turns one decision question into N biased-but-complete option dossiers for downstream scoring.
questions: How does the runtime construct the unit dict and guarantee that constraints are grounded in real systems?
What is the exact contract with the sibling score_options reducer for aggregating and recommending across panel outputs?
How is the {'panel'} mode_scope cast applied by the execution engine, and what happens if an unknown lens is supplied?
Where is the composition code that calls run_items(develop_option, [...]) and run_reduce(mode='role')?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a single-role panelist that develops one biased approach per unit and emits a structured, echo-keyed dossier, while explicitly not deciding the fork.
apparent_themes: Advisory separation of option generation from scoring/recommendation
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a standardized, reusable panelist that can be fanned across a fixed set of reasoning lenses, producing diverse but comparable option dossiers so no single role monopolizes the option space and a downstream reducer can score them.
novelty: The lens-in-unit axis inversion that lets one role file serve as N independent panelists, combined with an explicit closed lens vocabulary declared as module data.
contribution: A standardized panelist that emits a flat, lens-keyed option dossier (approach, buys, costs, touches, risk) suitable for side-by-side scoring, plus a closed LENSES vocabulary and the lens-in-unit pattern that enables fanning one role across multiple biases.
summary_for_embedding: roles/develop_option.py defines the develop_option role and DevelopOptionOut schema for generating one lens-biased approach to a decision, fanned by run_items over a closed lens vocabulary as the MAP half of an option-panel composition.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a reusable MAP panelist that develops distinct, grounded, comparable option approaches from fixed lenses so a downstream reducer can score and recommend without the role itself choosing the fork.

symbols.does: Pydantic output schema for one lens-biased option dossier.
symbols.description: Defines six scalar string fields: lens (echoed from the input unit), approach, buys, costs, touches, and risk. The flat shape is designed so each panelist returns a clean, comparable record that a downstream score_options reducer can key by lens.
